one_line: Soft pastels (peach, sky, sage) on cream. Hand-crafted feel without literal illustrations. Warmth as posture. Feels like Loops, Read.cv, Notion marketing, Substack.

background.role: Warm off-white. Slightly more cream than editorial-premium.

surface_peach.role: Pastel peach panel — for hero halves, accent sections.

surface_blush.role: Pastel blush — fourth accent panel. Use SPARINGLY — picking too many pastels at once gets candy-shop.

text_primary.role: Warm dark — almost-black with warmth, never pure #000.

accent.role: Warm terracotta — primary accent. Pairs with all pastels.

accent_alt.role: Sage — alternative accent. Pick ONE primary.

rules: Pastels are PANEL backgrounds, not button colors. Buttons are accent terracotta (or accent_alt sage) — saturated, not pastel
Use ONE pastel surface per section. Stacking peach + sky + sage in adjacent sections makes it candy-shop
Background is warm off-white (#FCF8F3), NOT pure white. Pure white loses the warmth
Text is warm dark (#2A2520) — pure #000 reads cold and wrong here

display.family: Söhne, Inter Display, Inter, -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif
display.rule: Sans-serif display, weights 600-700. This profile is sans throughout — the warmth comes from palette + layout, not from serif type. Loops, Read.cv, Notion marketing all do this.

decorative_serif.family: Editorial New, Tiempos Headline, Georgia, serif
use_for: ONE pull-quote per page, in italics. NOT for hero headlines. Serif here is a flourish, not the main type.
decorative_serif.rule: Optional. Use only if there's a specific moment where italic serif elevates a single quote or testimonial.

rules: Sans-serif headlines — this is NOT editorial-premium. Inter or Söhne weight 600-700, tight tracking
Generous line-height (1.6 body, 1.1 hero) — part of the warmth
Use casual punctuation: em-dashes, ampersands. Avoid corporate parallelism in headlines
Generous — closer to editorial-premium than vercel-dev
Lots of vertical breathing room is part of the joy
DEFAULT radius is larger here: md = 12px (vs 6-8px in other profiles)
Cards: lg (20px). Hero pastel panels: xl (32px) for that signature curved-corner feel
Buttons: md (12px) — slightly rounder than other profiles, but still not pill-shaped
Subtle warm shadows allowed here: 0 4px 12px rgba(42,37,32,0.06)
Pastel panels can layer subtly via different pastel hues — peach over sage, etc.
Avoid aggressive shadows — this is soft, not dramatic

button_primary.rule: Saturated terracotta on cream. The button is the warmth payoff.

card_with_pastel.rule: Pastel panel cards are a signature. Use them for testimonials, feature highlights, CTA blocks.

marketing_hero.structure: Centered hero, max-width 760px. Sans-serif headline (weight 700, 5xl-6xl, tight tracking). Friendly subhead. ONE terracotta CTA + one ghost. Below: a pastel-peach feature panel or testimonial card.
do: Real product imagery if available — or a clean abstract shape in pastel
Conversational copy ('Made for the way you actually work')
dont: Multiple pastels in the hero — pick one

landing_features.structure: 3-column grid (1-column mobile), each card uses a different pastel surface. Inside each: title (xl weight 600) + 2-sentence description.
do: Vary the pastel per card (peach / sky / sage)
Optional: a friendly Lucide icon at the top

login.structure: Centered card (radius lg, white surface) on a peach or sky pastel background. Title in display weight 600. Form fields with rounded inputs.
do: One pastel as the page background
dont: Split-screen with marketing copy (that's editorial-premium territory)

animations.library: gsap with back.out and elastic easings
animations.philosophy: Friendly and gentle. Bouncing, not snapping. But not childish.

patterns.hover: Card lifts 4-6px with shadow on hover, 300ms back.out(1.4).
patterns.button_press: Slight scale(0.96) bounce, then release with elastic.
patterns.hero_intro: Stagger fade-up on hero elements, 500ms each with 100ms stagger, back.out(1.4) easing.
patterns.icon_idle: Optional: subtle 4s loop on hero icons — float, breathe, micro-rotation. Restrained.
